items.q: What food is made by fermenting milk with bacterial cultures?
items.explanation: Yogurt forms when specific bacteria ferment milk sugars into lactic acid, thickening the milk and giving it a tangy flavor.

items.q: Spiders have six legs.
items.explanation: Spiders are arachnids, and arachnids have eight legs rather than six.

items.q: What is the name of the school in the Harry Potter series?
items.explanation: Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry is where Harry, Hermione, and Ron study in J. K. Rowling's series.

items.q: What sport is played with a cue ball on a table with pockets?
items.explanation: In pool, players use cues to strike a cue ball and pocket the object balls on a cloth-covered table.

items.q: What board game features suspects, rooms, and murder weapons to solve a mystery?
items.explanation: Clue, called Cluedo in some regions, revolves around deducing the culprit, location, and weapon.

items.q: Who wrote the children's novel Charlotte's Web?
items.explanation: Charlotte's Web was published in 1952 and follows Wilbur the pig and the spider Charlotte.

items.q: A rectangle always has four right angles.
items.explanation: Having four right angles is part of the definition of a rectangle, whether or not all four sides are equal.

items.q: What material is folded to create traditional origami models?
items.explanation: Origami is the Japanese art of paper folding and typically avoids cuts and glue in traditional practice.

items.q: What is the name of the process where water vapor cools and becomes liquid droplets?
items.explanation: Condensation forms dew and clouds when moist air cools to its dew point.

items.q: What family board game asks players to draw and guess words or phrases?
items.explanation: Pictionary is a party game where teams earn points by correctly identifying sketches before time runs out.

items.q: What is the medical term for fear of enclosed spaces?
items.explanation: Claustrophobia is a specific phobia involving enclosed or cramped spaces such as elevators, tunnels, or small rooms.

items.q: Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature.
items.explanation: Mercury is liquid at ordinary room temperature, while metals such as gallium melt only at slightly warmer temperatures.

items.q: Which novel features Captain Ahab's pursuit of a white whale?
options: The Old Man and the Sea
items.explanation: Herman Melville's Moby-Dick was published in 1851 and centers on Ahab's obsessive quest.

items.q: Which painter is best known for the Water Lilies series?
items.explanation: Monet painted many Water Lilies works at his garden in Giverny over several decades.

items.q: Which chemical element has the atomic number 47?
items.explanation: Silver's symbol is Ag, from the Latin word argentum.

items.q: What secure web protocol is indicated by a padlock icon in modern browsers?
items.explanation: HTTPS uses TLS encryption to protect data in transit between browser and server.

items.q: Absolute zero is the theoretical temperature at which molecular motion reaches a minimum.
items.explanation: Absolute zero is defined as 0 kelvin, the lower limit of thermodynamic temperature.

items.q: Which sea lies between Jordan and Israel?
items.explanation: The Dead Sea is a hypersaline lake in the Jordan Rift Valley and its shoreline is one of the lowest land elevations on Earth.

items.q: Which artist created The Great Wave off Kanagawa?
items.explanation: The woodblock print is part of Hokusai's series Thirty-six Views of Mount Fuji.

items.q: Which country won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
items.explanation: Uruguay hosted the inaugural tournament and defeated Argentina in the final.

items.q: What branch of theology seeks to justify divine goodness in the face of evil?
items.explanation: Theodicy addresses the problem of evil by asking how suffering and evil can coexist with an all-good, all-powerful deity.

items.q: The Domesday Book was compiled after the Norman Conquest of England.
items.explanation: William the Conqueror ordered the Domesday survey in 1085 to record landholding and resources in England.

items.q: What musical form typically consists of an exposition, development, and recapitulation?
items.explanation: Sonata form became central to many first movements in the Classical period, especially in symphonies and sonatas.

items.q: What astronomical term describes the point where the Moon crosses the ecliptic moving northward?
items.explanation: The Moon's ascending node is one of the two points where its orbit crosses the ecliptic plane from south to north.

items.q: What rhetorical term means deliberate understatement for effect?
items.explanation: Litotes often creates emphasis by expressing an idea through negation, such as saying not bad to mean very good.

items.q: Which philosopher wrote Leviathan?
items.explanation: Hobbes published Leviathan in 1651 as a foundational work of political philosophy centered on sovereignty and social order.

items.q: A quasar is powered by matter falling into a supermassive black hole.
items.explanation: Quasars are extremely bright active galactic nuclei fueled by accretion onto supermassive black holes.

items.q: What economic condition combines inflation with stagnant growth?
items.explanation: Stagflation is especially difficult for policymakers because inflation and weak growth usually call for conflicting responses.

items.q: Which painter created Las Meninas?
items.explanation: Las Meninas, painted in 1656, is housed in the Prado Museum and is known for its complex perspective.

items.q: In economics, what term means the value of the next best alternative forgone?
items.explanation: Opportunity cost captures trade-offs by measuring what is given up when one option is chosen over another.
